root_text.sn56.41:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn56.41:1.3: “bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, aññataro puriso rājagahā nikkhamitvā ‘lokacintaṁ cintessāmī’ti yena sumāgadhā pokkharaṇī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sumāgadhāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre nisīdi lokacintaṁ cintento.
root_text.sn56.41:1.4: Addasā kho, bhikkhave, so puriso sumāgadhāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ bhisamuḷālaṁ pavisantaṁ.
root_text.sn56.41:1.7: Yaṁ loke natthi taṁ mayā diṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.sn56.41:2.1: Atha kho so, bhikkhave, puriso nagaraṁ pavisitvā mahājanakāyassa ārocesi:
root_text.sn56.41:2.3: Yaṁ loke natthi taṁ mayā diṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.sn56.41:2.4: ‘Kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, ambho purisa, ummatto kathaṁ viceto?
root_text.sn56.41:2.5: Kiñca loke natthi yaṁ tayā diṭṭhan’ti?
root_text.sn56.41:2.6: ‘Idhāhaṁ, bhante, rājagahā nikkhamitvā “lokacintaṁ cintessāmī”ti yena sumāgadhā pokkharaṇī tenupasaṅkamiṁ; upasaṅkamitvā sumāgadhāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre nisīdiṁ lokacintaṁ cintento.
root_text.sn56.41:2.7: Addasaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, sumāgadhāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ bhisamuḷālaṁ pavisantaṁ.
root_text.sn56.41:2.8: Evaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, ummatto evaṁ viceto.
root_text.sn56.41:2.9: Idañca loke natthi yaṁ mayā diṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.sn56.41:2.10: ‘Taggha tvaṁ, ambho purisa, ummatto taggha viceto.
root_text.sn56.41:2.11: Idañca loke natthi yaṁ tayā diṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.sn56.41:3.1: Taṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, so puriso bhūtaṁyeva addasa, no abhūtaṁ.
root_text.sn56.41:3.3: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, saṅgāme devā jiniṁsu, asurā parājiniṁsu.
root_text.sn56.41:3.4: Parājitā ca kho, bhikkhave, asurā bhītā bhisamuḷālena asurapuraṁ pavisiṁsu devānaṁyeva mohayamānā.
root_text.sn56.41:4.2: ‘sassato loko’ti vā ‘asassato loko’ti vā, ‘antavā loko’ti vā ‘anantavā loko’ti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’ti vā ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’ti vā, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.
root_text.sn56.41:4.4: Nesā, bhikkhave, cintā atthasaṁhitā nādibrahmacariyakā na nibbidāya na virāgāya na nirodhāya na upasamāya na abhiññāya na sambodhāya na nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn56.41:5.1: Cintentā kho tumhe, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti cinteyyātha …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti cinteyyātha.
root_text.sn56.41:5.3: Esā, bhikkhave, cintā atthasaṁhitā esā ādibrahmacariyakā esā nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn56.41:6.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.41:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn56.41:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.41:1.3: “Once upon a time, mendicants, a certain person left Rājagaha, thinking ‘I’ll speculate about the world.’ They went to the Sumāgadhā lotus pond and sat down on the bank speculating about the world.
translation_text.sn56.41:1.4: Then that person saw an army of four divisions enter a lotus stalk.
translation_text.sn56.41:1.5: When he saw this he thought,
translation_text.sn56.41:1.6: ‘I’ve gone mad, really, I’ve lost my mind!
translation_text.sn56.41:1.7: I’m seeing things that don’t exist in the world.’
translation_text.sn56.41:2.1: Then that person entered the city and informed a large crowd,
translation_text.sn56.41:2.2: ‘I’ve gone mad, really, I’ve lost my mind!
translation_text.sn56.41:2.3: I’m seeing things that don’t exist in the world.’
translation_text.sn56.41:2.4: ‘But how is it that you’re mad? How have you lost your mind?
translation_text.sn56.41:2.5: And what have you seen that doesn’t exist in the world?’
translation_text.sn56.41:2.6: ‘Sirs, I left Rājagaha, thinking “I’ll speculate about the world.” I went to the Sumāgadhā lotus pond and sat down on the bank speculating about the world.
translation_text.sn56.41:2.7: Then I saw an army of four divisions enter a lotus stalk.
translation_text.sn56.41:2.8: That’s why I’m mad, that’s why I’ve lost my mind.
translation_text.sn56.41:2.9: And that’s what I’ve seen that doesn’t exist in the world.’
translation_text.sn56.41:2.10: ‘Well, my friend, you’re definitely mad, you’ve definitely lost your mind.
translation_text.sn56.41:2.11: And you’re seeing things that don’t exist in the world.’
translation_text.sn56.41:3.1: But what that person saw was in fact real, not unreal.
translation_text.sn56.41:3.2: Once upon a time, a battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.sn56.41:3.3: In that battle the gods won and the titans lost.
translation_text.sn56.41:3.4: The defeated and terrified titans entered the citadel of the titans through the lotus stalk only to confuse the gods.
translation_text.sn56.41:4.1: So mendicants, don’t speculate about the world.
translation_text.sn56.41:4.2: For example: the cosmos is eternal, or not eternal, or finite, or infinite; the soul and the body are one and the same, or the soul is one thing, the body another; after death, a realized one still exists, or no longer exists, or both still exists and no longer exists, or neither still exists nor no longer exists.
translation_text.sn56.41:4.4: Because those thoughts aren’t beneficial or relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life. They don’t lead to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.41:5.1: When you think something up, you should think: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn56.41:5.3: Because those thoughts are beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life. They lead to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.41:6.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.41:1.4: caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ → pokkharaṇiyā caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ (bj); caturaṅginisenaṁ (mr) | bhisamuḷālaṁ → bhisamūḷālaṁ (pts1ed); bhisamūlālaṁ (mr)
